name: Red Lettuce Quarters with Oranges
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Lettuce head, 6-8 oz (red oak leaf, leaf, or romaine)
2 tsp Canned green peppercorns, drained and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the lettuce lengthwise through the core into quarters.
2. Immerse the lettuce in water and gently swish to remove dirt; shake gently to remove water. Wrap in towels, slip into plastic bags, and chill for 30 minutes or up to a day.
3. Grate enough orange peel to make 1/2 teaspoon. Cut the remaining peel and white membrane off the oranges.
4. Over a bowl, cut between the membranes to remove the orange sections. Squeeze the membrane over the bowl to drain all the juice; you need about 3 tablespoons (save extra juice for another use).
5. Whisk together the 3 tablespoons of orange juice, orange peel, mayonnaise, vinegar, honey, and peppercorns. If made ahead, cover and chill the fruit and dressing separately up to 4 hours.
6. On each of 4 salad or dinner plates, place 1 wedge of lettuce. Top with equal portions of fruit and dressing.
